sections.heading: Get MongoDB Data into Parquet
sections.body:
Use Atlas Data Federation to get data in any format from your Atlas cluster or
S3 (Simple Storage Service) Bucket in to Apache Parquet, which allows you to query data in
the cloud quickly.

To learn more, see [Get MongoDB data into Parquet](atlas/data-federation/supported-unsupported/data-formats/parquet-data-files).

sections.heading: Run Queries on Many MongoDB Databases
sections.body:
Use Atlas Data Federation to query data across multiple databases, collections, and
Atlas clusters. To learn more, see [Query a Federated Database Instance](atlas/data-federation/query/query-federated-database).

sections.heading: Write Federated Queries Output to AWS S3
sections.body:
Query data from multiple sources and write the results of those queries
to an AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket using Atlas Data Federation [$out](adf-out-stage) syntax.
To learn more, see [$out Stage for Data Federation](atlas/data-federation/supported-unsupported/pipeline/out).

sections.heading: Copy Data from MongoDB to AWS S3
sections.body:
Use [Atlas Triggers](atlas-triggers) and Atlas Data Federation to write
data from your Atlas cluster to your AWS (Amazon Web Services) S3 (Simple Storage Service) bucket continuously
on a schedule.
To learn more, see [Scheduled Triggers](atlas/atlas-ui/triggers/scheduled-triggers).

sections.heading: Archive Data to Cloud Object Storage with Online Archive
sections.body:
Use MongoDB Atlas Online Archive to automatically archive data off
of your Atlas cluster to fully-managed cloud object storage.
To learn more, see [Archive data to cloud object storage using Online Archive](atlas/online-archive/configure-online-archive).
